score._comment: Score-only exclusions (rules still run + show in local CLI). Each entry is a reviewed false positive or a deliberate, documented won't-fix — not metric gaming. Code fixes were applied wherever genuinely safe.

_reasons.no-adjust-state-on-prop-change: 3 false positives: post-layout DOM measurement (TimecodeTextarea), IntersectionObserver visibility callback (miniaturePreview), external-store subscribe-sync (useRealtimeAudioLevels). None mirror a React prop.
_reasons.socket/low-supply-chain-score: jspdf 4.1.0 accepted: standard client-side PDF lib, core to export, no drop-in replacement. Kept installed.
_reasons.js-tosorted-immutable: 7 false positives: every flagged .sort() runs on a fresh [...] spread copy, never on props/state.
_reasons.js-set-map-lookups: 2 false positives: one is String.prototype.includes (substring), the other a one-off lookup on a distinct per-item array.
_reasons.js-index-maps: 1 false positive: lookup target (store.availableBaremes) is re-read and mutated mid-loop, so a precomputed Map would go stale.
_reasons.rerender-lazy-ref-init: 9 won't-fix: this project uses the React Compiler (react-hooks/preserve-manual-memoization). A custom lazy-ref hook is not recognized as a stable ref and breaks memoization preservation (hard lint errors). useRef(new Map()) is idiomatic; the saved alloc is negligible.
